task.name: Restore BMAD Config
task.description: Restore BMAD module config to default values after RBTV workflow completion
task.objective: Restore a BMAD module's config.yaml file (e.g., bmm/config.yaml) to its default output folder paths, reverting changes made by update-bmad-config task. This allows BMAD to return to its standard root {bmad_output} folder after RBTV project-specific workflows complete.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
Use standard BMAD defaults for restoration.
Preserve all other config values — only update output folder paths.

inputs.input: BMAD module whose config to restore (e.g., "bmm", "cis", "core"). Defaults to "bmm".

substep.title: Set Module Default
substep.action: Set target_module to "bmm" if not provided.

substep.title: Locate Config File
substep.action: Verify the config file exists at this path.

check.action: HALT: Report that config file does not exist. Cannot restore.

substep.title: Set Standard BMAD Defaults
substep.action: Define default values for BMAD module config:

substep.title: Read Current Config
substep.action: Note current values for logging purposes.

substep.title: Write Restored Config
substep.action: Replace output_folder, planning_artifacts, and implementation_artifacts with default values.
Preserve all other config values and formatting.

substep.title: Verify Restoration
substep.action: Read the updated config.yaml to verify defaults were applied.
Confirm all three values now use standard BMAD paths.

substep.title: Report Success
substep.action: " BMAD workflows will now use standard root output folder."

halt-conditions.condition: Target config file does not exist — report path and exit.
Write operation fails — report error and exit without completing restoration.

task.usage-note: Run this task after completing RBTV workflows that invoked BMAD components. This ensures subsequent BMAD usage (outside RBTV context) uses the standard output folders. Can be called manually or automatically at RBTV milestone completion.
